title: As he prepares to meet Xi, Trump no longer pushes for change in China’s system
description: In his first term, the president demanded fundamental shifts in China’s economic model. Now, he has copied parts of how Beijing does business.

title: He’s a billionaire running as a class traitor. Will Democrats buy it?
description: Tom Steyer is running for California governor on an affordability agenda, testing voters’ willingness to set aside their aversion to billionaires.

title: The complicated history of American presidents visiting China
description: From Nixon visiting during the Cold War to Clinton navigating the optics of being welcomed in Tiananmen Square, presidential trips to China have always been fraught.

title: Lawmaker indicted over migrant facility visit pushes to expand oversight
description: Rep. LaMonica McIver (D-New Jersey) plans to introduce a bill to strengthen scrutiny by members of Congress as her own legal battle is about to escalate.

title: As Trump goes to China, what do Americans say about tariffs, Iran and world standing?
description: Most Americans see China as one of the United States' biggest rivals or adversaries, but largely as an economic threat, according to a new Chicago Council/NPR/Ipsos poll.

title: Trump goes to China as Iran war smolders
description: President Trump says he has a great relationship with President Xi. His trip to China will provide a temperature check.

title: Voters head to the polls in Nebraska as Democrats hope to turn a red district blue
description: Nebraska voters head to the polls for primary elections Tuesday. Democrats in the traditionally red state see an opportunity to turn a GOP-controlled district blue.

title: Virginia Democrats appeal to U.S. Supreme Court to save new House maps
description: Some top Virginia Democrats express little hope that the appeal will affect this November’s congressional elections and instead will focus on running in existing districts.

title: Supreme Court hands Alabama major boost in redistricting fight
description: Alabama legislators are hoping to boost the GOP in November’s midterm elections in the wake of last month’s Supreme Court decision weakening the Voting Rights Act.

title: House ethics panel pledges ‘full and thorough’ investigation of Rep. Cory Mills
description: The Republican, whom President Donald Trump has endorsed for another term, has denied domestic violence and other allegations and sought to portray them as politically motivated.

title: Alito extends temporary order allowing mail distribution of abortion pill
description: The justices are weighing whether to uphold an appeals court decision that means patients would need to pick up the pill in person.

title: Trump taps former FEMA director to lead the disaster agency again
description: Cameron Hamilton led FEMA briefly in 2025. He was removed by the Trump administration after telling Congress that the agency should continue to exist. Now, he's been nominated to lead it once again.

title: Nonprofit sues the federal government over plans to paint Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool blue
description: The Cultural Landscape Foundation, a nonprofit advocacy organization, is asking a federal judge to halt President Trump's plans to resurface the reflecting pool on the National Mall.

title: Trump seeks to pause federal gas tax as prices soar amid Iran conflict
description: Congress would have to approve the proposal, and passing the savings on to drivers, rather than oil companies, would be difficult to enforce.

title: Nonprofit sues to stop Trump’s changes to Reflecting Pool, a historic site
description: President Donald Trump’s order to apply a blue-tinted surface to the Lincoln Memorial’s Reflecting Pool needed federal review, a D.C.-area foundation says.

title: In turf battle over AI, U.S. spy agencies vie for more sway than Commerce
description: As the White House grapples with cybersecurity threats from advanced artificial intelligence models, national security officials want more sway in AI regulation.

title: Opposition leader Maria Corina Machado talks about her plans to return to Venezuela
description: <>NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Venezuelan opposition leader Maria Corina Machado.

title: New poll finds a majority of Americans unsure if attempts on Trump's life were real
description: When given the options of "true," "false" or "not sure," and asked whether each of the incidents "was staged," a majority of respondents said they thought each event was either staged or were unsure.

title: Abortion pill by mail allowed for at least 3 more days, the Supreme Court says
description: Full access to the abortion pill mifepristone, including through telemedicine and the mail, will continue for at least three more days, the high court said on Monday.

title: Trump says Iran ceasefire is on ‘life support,’ calls latest proposal ‘garbage’
description: Iran defended the proposal as “reasonable and generous,” as oil prices rose Monday morning in response to the continued stalemate.

title: Trump wants to suspend the federal gas tax as prices soar amid war with Iran
description: Suspending the federal gas tax would require an act of Congress.

title: GOP redistricting splintering Florida community angers Republicans and Democrats
description: Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) said population growth necessitated redistricting Florida’s House map. But an overhaul of a majority-Hispanic area sparked a backlash.

title: Confident in China’s power, Xi is ready to host an unpredictable Trump
description: Holding no illusions about making lasting deals at this week’s summit, China’s leader looks to project Beijing as an alternative to U.S. volatility on the world stage.

title: Oregon's most unexpected gubernatorial candidate? A pencil with a point
description: Oregon's public schools rank last in fourth-grade reading, according to an analysis of national testing. As a wake-up call for elected leaders, Pencil is running for governor as a write-in candidate.

title: Abraham Foxman, influential leader of Anti-Defamation League, dies at 86
description: As the ADL’s national director for nearly 30 years, he battled antisemitism and other forms of prejudice, building the group into a powerful watchdog.

title: Lawyers for the global elite aren’t buying Trump’s gold card visa
description: Even Melania’s former lawyer is warning people away from Trump’s program designed to make immigration easier for the wealthy.
